finding: The split-hijack fix was incomplete: ProjectDetailPage is the structural twin of NewChatPage (same Stores.Chat.reset(), same conversation.created subscription, same navigate-on-create) and never reset SplitView. Its target route /projects/:id/chat/:cid renders the SAME ConversationPage, so the identical 'auto while split' reconcile replaces the focused pane.
verification: read ProjectDetailPage.tsx:195-211 and projects/module.tsx:74 — route element is ConversationPage (imported at :36 with a chat-extension-boundary-exception comment). Same mechanism as ITEM-7.
resolution: fixed — Stores.SplitView.reset() added beside the existing Stores.Chat.reset()

finding: max-w-[60%] on the right toolbar group is an unconditional RESERVATION, not a min-content guarantee. The left group is flex-1 with a zero basis, so it grows into whatever the ceiling leaves spare even when it holds only the 36px '+' button — truncating the model name far earlier than the row required and stranding an empty gutter mid-toolbar.
verification: measured at 390px: 64.4px of empty space between the '+' button and an already-ellipsized model name. Independently re-derived from the flex algorithm before the audit reported it.
resolution: fixed — replaced the right-group ceiling with a min-width FLOOR (min-w-9) on the left group, so it takes exactly the '+' button's footprint and the model name gets everything else. New TEST-4 assertion pins the gutter <= 24px; verified it FAILS (64.4px) with the ceiling restored.

finding: min-w-0 was added to the loaded branch's wrapper but not to the error branch's identical <div data-testid="model-selector">, whose kit Button is shrink-0 + whitespace-nowrap. With the right toolbar group now shrinkable, that content can overrun its flex item and collide with Send.
verification: reported independently by 4 of 4 audit agents; confirmed by reading ModelSelector.tsx:117-133 against kit shadcn/button.tsx:7 (base variant carries shrink-0 whitespace-nowrap).
resolution: fixed — min-w-0 added to the error branch wrapper with a comment. Note the FLOOR fix above also materially improves this case: the right group now receives row-minus-44px instead of 60% of the row.

finding: Moving the captions out of the kit Menu group made them SIBLINGS of the <nav>, so the text now sits outside every landmark: skipped by landmark navigation, and — being neither heading nor labelled region — invisible to heading-key skimming. The component's own docstring justified not using a heading on the grounds that the nav's aria-label names the section, but that argument only holds while the caption is INSIDE the region it labels, which this change undid.
verification: traced the new parent chain to #app-sidebar (sdk/packages/shell/src/layouts/AppLayout.tsx:496), a plain div. A second agent independently noted the recent-chats error/loading/empty branches render no labelled <ul> at all, so 3 of its 4 states had no accessible name for the section.
resolution: fixed — SidebarSectionTitle now renders an <h2>. Tailwind preflight strips default heading size/margin so the visual result is unchanged. NOTE: app-level axe could not be re-run here (the e2e harness needs docker), so this is verified by construction and by tsc/lint, not by an axe pass.

finding: The pre-send assertions (split-chat-view / chat-pane-1 toHaveCount(0)) are vacuous and the comment above them ('On main the split survives here') is false. /chat routes to NewChatPage, and the split DOM only ever exists under ConversationPage, so both assertions pass identically with and without the fix.
verification: module.tsx:63 maps /chat to NewChatPage; ConversationPage.tsx:123 is the only renderer of SplitChatView. The store state at that moment is not observable from the DOM.
resolution: fixed — removed the two vacuous assertions and the false claim; replaced with an explicit note that the discriminating assertions are the post-send ones, and why.

finding: TEST-7's row-equality loop was effectively a tautology: the 'nav ul > li > button' half only matches the three kit <Menu> instances, all built from the same menuRowClasses() string inside a px-2 <ul>, so they cannot disagree by construction. The only independently-styled rows (recent chats) are absent on a freshly-provisioned e2e account, and the rows.length >= 2 guard was satisfied by the nav Menu alone.
verification: read menuRowClasses() (kit/menu.tsx:90-97) against RecentConversationsWidget.tsx:270-284 — genuinely two independent styling sources; the widget's empty branch renders no rows.
resolution: fixed — the spec now seeds two conversations via the API, reloads, waits for a real recent row, and asserts recentRowCount >= 1 with a message explaining why the control is otherwise meaningless.

finding: pt-0 pb-0.5 halved the caption-to-row gap from the kit group title's 4px to 2px for Navigation/Tools, leaving the caption 2px above a full-bleed 32px hover/selected pill — below the design system's 4px base rhythm.
verification: kit menu.tsx:174 uses py-1 (4px); menuRowClasses().row paints bg-primary/bg-accent full-bleed.
resolution: fixed — pb-0.5 -> pb-1, restoring the 4px on-grid rhythm for all three captions.

finding: The ellipsis defect is in the KIT (shadcn/select.tsx:44 sets both line-clamp-1 and flex on every trigger's value slot), so all ~36 Select call sites in src/modules still hard-cut long values. Repairing it at one app call site leaves the defect everywhere else and forces every future author to rediscover the workaround.
verification: independently reported by 2 agents; the kit source confirms the class pair is on the shared trigger, not this call site.
resolution: NOT fixed here, deliberately. sdk/ is a separate submodule repository: changing it requires its own PR plus a pointer bump, and bumping the pointer to an unpushed commit would break every other clone. Recorded as the top follow-up recommendation and surfaced to the human.

finding: The comment claimed a <button> with width:auto 'ignores the space actually on offer'. Shrink-to-fit is min(max(min-content, available), max-content), so it does consult available space; the overflow actually happens because the trigger sets whitespace-nowrap, making min-content the full unwrapped label.
verification: the correction is right — the conclusion (do not use w-auto) is unaffected, but the stated mechanism was wrong and would mislead a future reader.
resolution: fixed — comment rewritten to name whitespace-nowrap as the real dependency.

finding: NewChatPage is the element for BOTH /chat and /, and / is the target of the router catch-all (<Navigate to="/"> for path="*") and the 403 page's 'Back to home' link. Neither is a new-chat intent, so an unknown URL or a permission bounce now also collapses the workspace.
resolution: NOT changed. / renders the same new-chat surface, so collapsing there is consistent with the approved invariant (DEC-7), and the alternative — gating the reset on the specific path — would reintroduce the per-entry-point fragility this fix exists to remove. Surfaced to the human as an accepted consequence.

finding: The invariant is asserted once in a mount effect, but SplitView hydrates behind an async dynamic import of Auth.store and re-runs hydrateFor() on auth-identity change; the persisted blob is only removed by a 250ms-debounced save. A reload inside that window can restore panes AFTER the reset has run.
verification: SplitView.store.ts:296-326 confirms the async hydration path and the 250ms scheduleSave debounce; reported independently by 2 agents.
resolution: NOT fixed here. The window requires a reload within ~250ms of clicking New Chat, and a robust fix means making hydration order-aware (a store-level concern touching every reset caller, not this route). Recorded as a known narrow race and surfaced to the human rather than absorbed silently.

finding: reset() also forces mode back to 'split', so a route mount now silently discards the user's tabs-vs-split workspace preference — the first reset caller that fires without the workspace genuinely collapsing.
resolution: NOT fixed here — narrowing reset()'s contract affects its three existing callers and belongs with the store, not this fix. Surfaced to the human.

finding: The shared caption's typography (text-xs font-semibold tracking-wide) matches neither the kit Menu group caption still used by the settings rail (font-medium, no tracking) nor the pane-manager drawer's captions (font-medium uppercase tracking-wide), so the app now carries three treatments of the same semantic element.
verification: SettingsPage.tsx:83 still uses {type:'group'}; PaneManagerDrawer.tsx:172,228 use uppercase font-medium. The new component reproduces the pre-existing 'Recent chats' treatment exactly.
resolution: NOT changed. The typography was inherited verbatim from the caption the human explicitly asked the other two to match; unifying the settings rail and pane-manager captions is a wider design decision outside this PR's scope. Surfaced to the human with the concrete inventory.

finding: The rail's dominant TEXT edge is 20px (every menu label, chat title and the footer profile row); the captions now sit at 12px, an inset shared by nothing else. Before the change 2 of 3 captions were flush with that 20px text edge and only 'Recent chats' hung out — the fix normalized the two conforming captions onto the outlier rather than the reverse.
verification: inset arithmetic independently confirmed during planning and re-confirmed by the audit.
resolution: NOT changed — this is the human's explicit decision (DEC-4), taken from an option picker that showed both alignments with rendered previews and measurements. Per the audit-vs-user-decision rule it is recorded and surfaced, never silently reversed.

finding: The loader fix is safe for other consumers (verified by running the full suite under both versions: 10 failures -> 8, a strict subset), but its commit message's premise is only partly delivered — 8 of the 10 files now get past resolution and die on 'TypeScript enum is not supported in strip-only mode', so npm run test:unit still exits non-zero.
verification: an audit agent re-ran the suite under both hook versions independently and reproduced 10 -> 8.
resolution: Accurate, and already stated as such in the commit body and INFRA_INTEGRATION.md. The residual 8 need enum removal from product source or a real transpiler — out of scope. TEST-1 is run and reported individually rather than via the suite's exit code.

finding: SOFT_CEILING_PX=320 and OLD_FIXED_CAP_PX=130 hardcode the pixel translations of the very class strings the spec header says it deliberately does not assert, and 20rem->320px assumes a 16px root font nothing pins.
resolution: NOT changed. Both constants are bounds-checks, not equality assertions (the ceiling asserts <=, the old cap asserts >), so a re-tune to 22rem keeps TEST-2 green and only the ceiling check would need updating; the failure messages name the ceiling explicitly. Deriving them from the source would require importing app code into the visual specs, which the suite deliberately avoids.

finding: TEST-5's opt.scrollW <= opt.clientW assertion cannot currently fail: the popup is w-auto and SelectItem sets no overflow/max-width, so the option box always grows to its text.
verification: kit select.tsx:199 (w-auto min-w-(--anchor-width)) and shadcn/select.tsx:131 confirm it.
resolution: Kept. It is a guard against a future kit change adding truncation to SelectItem — cheap, and the test's load-bearing assertion (opt.text contains the full name, run precisely while the trigger IS ellipsized) is not vacuous.

finding: TEST-1 does not touch NewChatPage — deleting Stores.SplitView.reset() from the page leaves it green, so it adds no independent coverage of the fix itself.
verification: correct: the test calls s().reset() directly.
resolution: Kept as-is and re-labelled honestly in TEST_RESULTS.md. It proves the store-level transition composes; the fix's own wiring is covered by TEST-8 (e2e). The unit tier cannot mount a React page in this repo (node --test, no jsdom), which is exactly why TEST-8 exists.

finding: Verified CLEAN by an independent agent against Node v22.23.1: err.url is set by finalizeResolution for both handled error codes and NOT by the bare-package packageResolve throw, so the !err.url guard correctly rethrows genuinely-missing packages; realpathSync creates no dual module identity because Node ESM already realpaths by default.
resolution: no action

finding: Verified CLEAN by two independent agents: no test, spec, source selector or CSS depends on the removed *-menu-group-N testids; the kit Menu never received a collapsed prop at this call site, so icon-only rendering is unchanged; there is no desktop override of LeftSidebar carrying security-relevant logic.
resolution: no action

finding: Verified CLEAN: the in-split 'new chat pane' flow goes through ConversationPickerPane (local state only, never mounts this route); both existing callers that navigate to /chat already call reset() first, so the idempotence claim holds; the desktop pop-out route has its own store and is unaffected.
resolution: no action
